title: England Exposes the All Blacks and Chris Jones Doesn’t Hold Back

England’s comeback at Twickenham wasn’t a one off to him. It was a pattern New Zealand keeps repeating, and he can’t believe we still haven’t learned how to close out a game. We talk through the recurring issues: losing momentum, basic skill errors, yellow cards, a forward pack going missing after halftime, and a coaching group Razor admits is still “learning on the job.” Chris can’t fathom that at this level. Neither can we. He also looks at leadership gaps, and why the All Blacks look anything but sure of themselves after 12 tests this year. From Argentina’s guts, to South Africa thriving with 14 men, to Wales’ struggles, he gives us the full Northern picture. Plus, Belgium are one win away from a Rugby World Cup. Yes, really. title: Tony Kemp: Warriors MUST Beat Manly for NRL Finals Momentum
summary: Former Warriors coach Tony Kemp joins Martin Devlin to break down the Warriors’ recent defeat to the Parramatta Eels, and what it means for their push towards the NRL finals. Kempy highlights how Mitchell Moses’ kicking game proved decisive for the Eels, while stressing the Warriors must dominate through the middle if they want to compete with powerhouses like Penrith and Canberra come finals time. Kempy says coaching will ultimately decide this year’s premiership, with Ivan Cleary, Craig Bellamy, and Ricky Stuart looming large. title: Sean Fitzpatrick on Historic 1996 South Africa Series Win & State of Current All Blacks
summary: Former All Blacks captain and rugby legend Sean Fitzpatrick joins Martin Devlin on the DSPN, to reflect on one of the greatest All Blacks achievements - the historic test series win in South Africa in 1996. Fitzpatrick recalls the bonds formed on that tour, the emotional final whistle in Pretoria, and the pride in achieving something generations of All Blacks had chased since 1921. He shares memories of legends like Zinzan Brooke, Christian Cullen, and coach John Hart, while also assessing today’s side under Scott Robertson. Fitzpatrick believes the All Blacks are building well, but stresses the expectation to win every test remains central to New Zealand rugby’s identity. title: All Blacks vs Springboks - Ranking the Greatest Tests in Rugby's Fiercest Rivalry
summary: Sky Sport rugby commentator Tony Johnson joins Martin Devlin on the DSPN to relive the greatest rivalry in world rugby - All Blacks vs Springboks. From memories of transistor radios and his father’s tales of Lancaster Park in 1956, to the drama of Loftus in 1996, the heartbreak of World Cup finals in 1995 and 2023, and epic contests in Soweto, Cape Town, and at Ellis Park - TJ captures the intensity, history, and respect that defines the clash. He and Marty recall the greatest New Zealand triumphs, and the most gut-wrenching defeats. title: Springboks 'Unbeatable', Wallabies Mind Games, & Eden Park's Record | Rugby Championship Preview
summary: In a special edition of the DSPN, Martin Devlin is joined by two good mates - Brett McKay and Harry Jones, hosts of The 8-9 Combo Rugby Podcast. The trio endeavour on a spirited, and slightly cheeky, Rugby Championship preview, with the first two matches on this weekend. They talk all sorts including the mad-scientist tinkering of Springboks coach Rassie Erasmus, South Africa's depth and whether it makes them unbeatable (spoiler: it doesn’t), and Wallabies coach Joe Schmidt’s tactical mind games. There’s banter about underdog claims, Australia's chances, and the curious art of lifting players in open play. While South Africa might be favourites, Brett and Harry reckon surprises are on the cards - especially at Ellis Park or Eden Park. There's also a look at Australia’s big test against South Africa, and why the Wallabies may be walking into the toughest environment in world rugby. title: Tony Kemp: 'Time to Panic' After NZ Warriors' 3rd Loss in A Row | 2025 NRL Season
summary: On the DSPN, Martin Devlin delivers a sermon the N Z Warriors losing ANOTHER game - their third in a row and fifth in seven - going down to the Canterbury Bulldogs 32-14 in the NRL. Is the top four dream over? Could this team be in danger of slipping out of the top six? Then, Marty and ex Warriors coach Tony Kemp dive into the club's worrying slide, with their once-comfortable finals buffer now under threat. Kempy reflects on injuries, the mental and physical toll of the season, and the dangers of relying too heavily on missing players like Mitch Barnett. He warns that current form won’t cut it against determined sides like the Dragons, who are finding momentum. Both agree the only focus now should be securing a win on Friday night - ugly or not - to keep the finals push alive and restore confidence. title: Wake-Up Call for NZ Warriors? Tony Kemp Breaks Down Shock Loss to Titans | 2025 NRL Season
summary: Martin Devlin and Tony Kemp - the former Warriors coach and Kiwis representative - are back talking Warriors on the DSPN! Together they break down the Wahs’ shock 24-16 loss to the Gold Coast Titans and what it means for the run home. Kempy explains why the Titans’ completion rate and smart kicking game exposed the Warriors, and dives deep into defensive structures - especially the much-talked-about right edge issues. He stresses that fatigue from poor ruck control, not just individual errors, is the real problem. The pair also look ahead at a tough six-game stretch, with the Dolphins clash now a must-win to keep top-four hopes alive. Can the Warriors find their form and stay in the hunt? title: Scrum Guru Mike Cron: Working with Wallabies, All Blacks Success, Case for Player Rest & Rotation
summary: On the DSPN, Martin Devlin chats with legendary scrum coach Mike Cron about his new book Lessons from an All Black Legend, which dives into life inside the All Blacks camp and the mindset behind elite performance. Cron reflects on the current state of the New Zealand national side, the growing gap between Super Rugby and international level, and why confidence is as crucial as technique in the scrum. He shares fascinating insights on managing player workloads, the evolution of the front row, and the art of coaching under high stakes. He also opens up about transitioning from the police force to rugby, the challenge of coaching the Black Ferns on short notice, and why staying humble - and hungry - is key to sustained success. title: 'Letter of the Law' - Christy Doran on Wallabies-Lions 2nd Test & Controversial Try That Was Awarded
summary: Christy Doran, rugby editor at The Roar in Sydney, joins Martin Devlin on the DSPN, to break down a Wallabies-British & Irish Lions epic overshadowed by that controversial call on 80 minutes. Christy believes, by the letter of the law, it was a penalty against Jac Morgan, and the winning try for the Lions shouldn't have stood. He reflects on the Wallabies’ brilliance in the first half, the costly Tom Lynagh knock-on, and Australia's inability to finish strong. Marty and Christy both feel Will Skelton and Rob Valetini made a huge impact, but depth and consistency remain Australia’s Achilles heel. title: Wallabies vs Lions - Did the Refs Get it Right? Tony Johnson on Late Controversy in Melbourne
summary: Longtime Sky Sport rugby commentator Tony Johnson joins Martin Devlin to dissect a Wallabies-British & Irish Lions test that had everything - brilliant rugby, controversy, and drama to the final whistle. TJ explains why the last-minute call on a potential illegal cleanout by the Lions was a true 50-50, and why overturning the try would have been monumental. Despite Australia’s explosive start, driven by the impact of Will Skelton and Rob Valetini, the Lions were able to turn the tide in the second 40. TJ highlights how Wallaby bench weakness and key errors proved costly in a game that could have gone either way. Beyond the Lions fallout, Tony and Marty preview NPC’s return, and share insights on the Black Ferns’ World Cup squad and why some big names missed out. title: Wallabies legend John Eales: Aus 'Need to Think Outside the Box' to beat British & Irish Lions
summary: Wallabies legend John Eales joins Martin Devlin on the DSPN for a candid rugby chat, following the first Australia-British & Irish Lions test, and ahead of the second tussle in Melbourne. John and Marty break down Australia’s first-test loss and what needs to change. John stresses the importance of mastering the basics - scrums, lineouts, and reducing costly mistakes - while highlighting confidence and mental resilience as key factors. He reckons the Aussies need to think more creatively and outside the box when attacking. John also praises coach Joe Schmidt’s leadership, but notes the Lions’ cohesion and intensity set a tough standard. title: All Blacks vs France - Mex Praises 'Smart' NZ Selectors & Backline, Defends Quality of French Rugby
summary: Martin Devlin and Murray Mexted go deep on the All Blacks’ recent test series win over France on the DSPN, with Mex firing up over “naive” analysis of the French side in the NZ media. Mexted backs the coaching trio of Scott Robertson, Jason Ryan and Scott Hansen, calling them “savvy and cunning", and says the team’s trending the right way... but the number eight jersey remains up for grabs. Argentina away looms large, and Mexted reckons it’ll be the biggest test yet for this evolving All Blacks side. summary: Sir Steve Hansen is back on the DSPN! The legendary All Blacks coach weighs in on New Zealand rugby, current coach Scott Robertson and the team's road ahead. Hansen also vents on World Rugby's messy rulebook and slow-moving global calendar, pushing for an independent group to simplify the game. From Fabian Holland’s engine to whether Japan’s top clubs could compete in Super Rugby Pacific, Sir Steve covers all things code! title: All Blacks vs France - What Did We Learn? Tony Johnson Unpacks NZ's 3-0 Test Series Win
summary: Longtime Sky Sport rugby commentator Tony Johnson joins Martin Devlin on the DSPN, to beak down the All Blacks' 29-19 win over France in the third and final test in Hamilton, and look at the 3-0 series win for New Zealand as a whole. TJ gives coach Scott Robertson and his players a B grade, praising the rise of lock Fabian Holland and the return of utility back Jordie Barret. But, the pair flag depth concerns at halfback, hooker, and midfield. Tony is optimistic but warns fans not to get carried away - France weren’t at full strength, and bigger tests are coming. The South Africans? Still the gold standard, and another good win for them on the weekend. The British and Irish Lions? Bullies against the Wallabies, and still with another gear or two in them. TJ and Marty wrap up talking about the under 20 final between South Africa and New Zealand, which the former won 23-15. title: Rugby Legend Nick Farr-Jones - Can Wallabies Topple British & Irish Lions in First Test?
summary: Wallabies legend and 1991 Rugby World Cup-winning captain Nick Farr-Jones joins Martin Devlin on the DSPN, to talk all about the test series against British and Irish Lions kicking off this weekend. Marty plays a game of NRL Glass Half Full with none other than Mr Positivity himself - Matt Gunn! The pair work through a number of clubs and whether they're optimistic or pessimistic about said clubs' form and fortunes. title: Tony Johnson, All Blacks Batter France, British & Irish Lions, Tight 5 | 14 July
summary: Welcome to episode 109 of the DSPN - Devlin Sports Podcast Network...   Tony Johnson - longtime rugby commentator for Sky Sport - is back on the show to review the All Blacks' 43-17 win over France in Wellington. The pair are fans of the much improved New Zealand performance, but know there is still room for improvement. TJ and Marty also look at other international fixtures over the weekend, including the British & Irish Lions and Springboks, who both played. After that, Marty and young producer Lachlan Waugh present the Tight 5, where they dissect and debate five separate sporting topics. Today's edition is all about the All Blacks and international rugby - the two talk about ABs selections for the third French test, the Australia-New Zealand invitational side losing heavily and embarrassingly to the Lions, the Springboks supposed innovative plays, and more.  All that and much more!     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

summary: DSPN producer Lachlan Waugh reviews the All Blacks' 43-17 win over France in the second rugby test in Wellington. New Zealand ran in six tries to two, in a performance highlighted by their forwards and set piece.     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: Murray Mexted APPLAUDS All Blacks' Backline, Questions Christian Lio-Willie Selection Ahead of 2nd France Test
summary: Former All Black, broadcasting icon, and a lover of all things RUGBY! Murray Mexted is back on the DSPN chatting with Martin Devlin, ahead of this weekend's second test against France in Wellington. The pair dive straight into it, sharing memories of Athletic Park and talking issues around refereeing and the TMO at international level. Mex also dissects the team named for the second clash - he reckons the All Blacks backline is the best he's seen in years, and loves that Billy Proctor has been retained despite the centre's defensive error in the first test. He doesn't quite agree with Christian Lio-Willie's selection, but is stoked Tupou Vaa'i is again picked at blindside flanker. Importantly, Mex and Marty want to see more maniacal passion for the All Blacks, given how significant the team is in New Zealand culture.     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: Jason Ryan, All Blacks vs France, Ant Strachan, Glass Half Full | July 10
summary: Welcome to episode 108 of the DSPN - Devlin Sports Podcast Network...   All Blacks assistant coach Jason Ryan joins the podcast for the very first time. He works through the rationale behind changes to the 23 for the second France rugby test, the pursuit of even BETTER set piece play, and how they plan to tackle the tricky weather expected in Wellington. Former All Blacks halfback Ant Strachan provides his thoughts on the ABs team named, as well as looking back at their performance in the first test. Marty and young producer Lachlan play a game of Glass Half Full - All Blacks style! They look at the team named, and provide optimism - or pessimism - on certain players and positions.  All that and much more!     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: All Blacks beat France, Tony Johnson, Scott Robertson, Brett McKay, Springboks | July 7
summary: Welcome to episode 106 of the DSPN - Devlin Sports Podcast Network...   Tony Johnson sits down for an extended chat, first covering off the All Blacks' 31-27 win over France in Dunedin, to begin their 2025 test season. TJ and Marty unpick all the important parts of the performance from New Zealand, as well as extending plenty of praise to a rotated French side. Marty and young producer Lachlan Waugh work through quotes of coach Scott Robertson post-match, and offer their thoughts on a variety of topics covered by Razor including the defence, Sevu Reece's head knock, and the frantic pace the All Blacks played at. Brett McKay - co-host of the 8-9 Combo Rugby Pod and writer for RugbyPass - talks Wallabies-Fiji and offers an Aussie perspective on the All Blacks win. We wrap up with part two of Marty's chat with TJ, where they recap the Springboks win over Italy and the British & Irish Lions nervy victory over the Waratahs.  All that and much more!     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: "I'm Delighted!" - Murray Mexted Applauds All Blacks Selectors after Naming Physical Side to Play France
summary: All Blacks icon and longtime rugby broadcaster Murray Mexted joins Martin Devlin on the DSPN, to share his satisfaction over the New Zealand side named to play France in the first of three tests on Saturday. Mex says the 23 is "very close" to the team he would have picked. He has long banged the drum for more size, and with the selection of Tupou Vaa'i at blindside flanker and inclusion of debutant Fabian Holland at lock, he reckons the selectors have finally "worked it out". Mex and Marty also talk about the need to physically dominate France, and the importance of snuffing them out of the contest entirely. Mex praises the Jordie Barrett-Billy Proctor midfield combo, reckons Du'Plessis Kirifi and Ardie Savea are the breakdown kings New Zealand needs, and cheekily suggests coach Razor might run the same side next week... if they do the smashing right.     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

summary: Welcome to episode 105 of the DSPN - Devlin Sports Podcast Network...   Veteran rugby broadcaster Nigel Yalden reviews the first All Blacks matchday side named for 2025 named, with the 23 unveiled today ahead of Saturday's opening test against France. Marty and Nigel work through the main talking points about the squad, including the loose forwards, Beauden Barrett at first-five, Rieko Ioane, and more. Marty and young producer Lachlan play a game of Glass Half Full, where they look at certain areas of the ABs team and share how positive (or negative) they are about those positions. Greg 'Marto' Martin is BACK talking all sorts of sport and men's things, relevant to blokes on both sides of the Tasman. Marto talks the likes of the Wallabies and Lions Series, the Brisbane Broncos, and strange radio promotions.  All that and much more!     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: Michael Witt, NZ Warriors, Matt Gunn, NRL Glass Half Full, Richie Morgan | 1 July
summary: Welcome to episode 104 of the DSPN - Devlin Sports Podcast Network...   Former Warriors half Michael Witt - who also played for the Eels, Sea Eagles and Dragons in the NRL - joins the podcast for the very first time, casting his eye over the Warriors' 26-12 defeat to the Broncos, Luke Metcalf's injury, and the team's push for a top four spot. Marty is joined by Matt Gunn - a diehard Bulldogs fan, who is probably the only Aussie to willingly relocate to Twizel - for a game of NRL Glass Half Full. The pair work through a number of teams and recent results, and offer their thoughts. Richie Morgan - Warriors diehard based across the ditch, and unofficial leader of Warrior Nation - talks through his endeavours travelling across Australia supporting his favourite team.  All that and much more!     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

summary: Welcome to episode 103 of the DSPN - Devlin Sports Podcast Network...   Tony Johnson form Sky Sport is back in studio, talking rrruuugggbbbyyy! The All Blacks team to play France is named on Thursday, so TJ and Marty predict who they think SHOULD be picked by coach Scott Robertson. They also cover off some international rugby matches that took place over the weekend. Marty and young producer Lachlan Waugh gather in the debating chamber like a couple of squabbling politicians, to discuss five separate sporting topics. They cover the Broncos' win over the Warriors in the NRL, Liam Lawson's best ever finish in a Formula One Grand Prix, and more. Christy Doran - Rugby Editor at The Roar in Sydney - covers the British & Irish Lions' 54-7 win over the Western Force, and wider storylines to do with the Lions' tour in Australia.  All that and much more!     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: Murray Mexted: All Blacks' Lack of Loose Forwards for French Series, Front Row Depth, and How to Beat South Africa
summary: All Blacks icon and former rugby broadcaster Murray Mexted joins DSPN host Martin Devlin, to review the naming of the first ABs squad of 22025. 33. Mex and Marty zero in on coach Scott Robertson naming only five loose forwards, questioning the height and aerial strength of the current loosie group, pointing out how vital it is to win possession - especially against towering northern hemisphere packs. Mex argues for players like Tupou Vaa’i should be trialed more at blindside flanker, and he highlights how rugby "turns men into better men". Mexted also believes Ardie Savea and Luke Jacobson should be considered as opensides. It's classic Murray Mexted - deep rugby insight, a few left-field calls, and plenty of passion.     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

summary: Welcome to episode 102 of the DSPN - Devlin Sports Podcast Network...   Crusaders coach Rob Penney joins Marty Devlin in a very special interview, talking all about his maiden Super Rugby title as coach of the Crusaders. Penney also chats about bouncing back from a tough 2024, and even admits he is still scarred from what went on last year. Marty and young producer Lachlan Waugh present the Tight 5! Where they dissect and debate five different sporting topics. Today's discussion includes the Lions and Barbarians matches on this weekend, and Zoe Hobbs setting another 100 metre record. Chris Jones from The Times and RugbyPass talks the British and Irish Lions series, with the first game on Australian soil on Saturday night.  All that and much more!     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: Ian Foster: Former All Blacks Coach on Despair of 2022 & Effect on his Family, World Cup Final, and New Book
summary: Former All Blacks coach Ian Foster joins Martin Devlin on the DSPN, to chat about his new book Leading Under Pressure - a raw, reflective look back at his turbulent tenure in charge of the New Zealand national side from 2020 to 2023. Foster opens up on the heartbreak of the World Cup final loss, the relentless public pressure, and how his family bore the brunt of the criticism. He talks candidly about support from others including Team New Zealand boss Grant Dalton, and the lack of support from New Zealand Rugby during the darkest moments. Foster shares thoughts on his successor Scott Robertson’s time so far, and doubles down on keeping top players in New Zealand. Light-hearted moments also pop up, including his pre-coaching radio career.     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: Tony Johnson: Crusaders 'Strangled' Chiefs with their 'Defensive DNA' in Super Rugby Pacific Final win
summary: In this lively post-final debrief, DSPN host Martin Devlin and Sky Sport rugby commentator Tony Johnson dissect the Crusaders’ gritty 16-12 Super Rugby Pacific win over the Chiefs. TJ insists the Chiefs didn’t choke, they just got their oxygen supply cut off! The Crusaders’ defensive DNA, home-ground muscle, and big-game smarts proved the difference. Marty tips his hat to Clayton McMillan’s Chiefs for restoring their mana, but laments their inability to close the deal - again. The pair also look back on a season packed with Aussie surprises, fast-paced footy, and referees finally letting games breathe.     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: Crusaders win Super Rugby Pacific - Tony Johnson, Steve Gordon, Geoff Parkes, Tight 5 | 23 June
summary: Welcome to episode 100 of the DSPN - Devlin Sports Podcast Network...   It's a BIG recap of the 2025 Super Rugby Pacific final, that saw the Crusaders beat the Chiefs 16-12 in Christchurch. Sky Sport rugby commentator Tony Johnson is first up reviewing the contest. Former All Blacks, Chiefs and Waikato lock Steve Gordon offers a perspective from the losing team. Marty and young producer Lachlan Waugh breeze through a Super Rugy-centric Tight 5. Geoff Parkes from The Roar in Melbourne gives an Australian take on the result, whether or not the right team won, and what it means for Aussie rugby.  All that and much more!     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: 'Still Good Enough for All Blacks' - Todd Blackadder on Richie Mo'unga and Crusaders-Chiefs Super Rugby Final
summary: In a very special interview on the DSPN, host Martin Devlin catches up with former All Blacks and Crusaders captain, and ex Crusaders coach Todd Blackadder, who recently guided his Brave Lupus to back-to-back Japanese League one titles. The pair dive into the form of Richie Mo'unga - who plays for Blackadder - with the coach convinced Mo'unga is still All Blacks material. Blackadder reflects on coaching lessons from his Crusaders days, including heartbreaking 2014 final defeat to the Waratahs, and the value of learning from losses. The pair preview Saturday’s Crusaders-Chiefs Super Rugby Pacific final, weighing up discipline, territory, and the power of set piece. Blackadder also backs the idea of a global club competition and shares a nostalgic nod to the Christchurch stadium’s swansong.     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: Todd Blackadder, Super Rugby, Sir Graham Lowe, State of Origin, Greg Martin | 19 June
summary: Welcome to episode 99 of the DSPN - Devlin Sports Podcast Network...   Sir Graham Lowe - winning Queensland Origin coach in 1991 - reviews game two in Perth, which was won by the Maroons over New South Wales 26-24. Former All Black and ex Crusaders coach Todd Blackadder previews this weekend's Super Rugby Pacific final, while also looking back on his title win with Brave Lupus in Japan's Rugby League One competition. Greg Martin gloats about the Queensland Origin victory, while also talking the Super Rugby final and the 2025 British and Irish Lions series.  All that and much more!     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: Murray Mexted: Chiefs Firepower 'Will Overwhelm' Crusaders' Defence in final | Super Rugby 2025
summary: DSPN host Martin Devlin welcomes back New Zealand rugby icon and former broadcaster Murray Mexted, as they attack this weekend's Super Rugby Pacific final between the crusaders and Chiefs in Christchurch. Mex is tipping the Chiefs to win, citing their superior firepower, highlighted by Shaun Stevenson, Emoni Narawa, and Damian McKenzie. While the Crusaders showed classic grit against the Blues, Mex questions their attacking threats beyond Will Jordan. He believes modern rugby is attack-driven, and the Chiefs are more likely to rack up points. The pair also touch on Scott Barrett likely being named All Blacks captain, praising his leadership and close bond with coach Scott Robertson. A nostalgic moment sees them recall Jonah Lomu’s legendary four-try performance in the 1995 Rugby World Cup semifinals. Mexted finishes by saying the Crusaders are gutsy, but this time the Chiefs have too many weapons.     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

summary: Welcome to episode 98 of the DSPN - Devlin Sports Podcast Network... Marty and young producer Lachlan Waugh discuss five separate sporting topics on the Tight 5, including the All Blacks team naming next week, and Crusaders CEO Colin Mansbridge banning cowbells for the Super final. Iain Payten from the Sydney Morning Herald offers an Aussie perspective on the Super Rugby decider, and chats the upcoming British and Irish Lions Tour, which - more or less - begins this weekend. Matt Gunn from Twizel is POSITIVE about State of Origin, the Bulldogs and Warriors, and hoar frosts. title: Rahul Patil: South Africa Breaks its Hoodo, as Australia Falls Apart in cricket's World Test Championship Final
summary: In this lively post-Test Championship debrief, DSPN host Martin Devlin welcomes cricket expert Rahul Patil onto the podcast, as they dissect South Africa's five-wicket win in the final at Lord's. Marty and Rahul revel in the Proteas' fairytale triumph, making gleeful digs at the Aussies' win-at-all-costs attitude. Rahul shines a light on South Africa’s emotional journey - from heartbreaks past to ultimate redemption. The pair unpack Aiden Markram’s monk-like composure, Temba Bavuma’s leadership, and why the win meant so much. They also lament poor batting techniques shaped by Twenty20 cricket and question the scheduling of the WTC Final straight after the IPL. With the Ashes looming and Australia ageing, Marty and Rahul also wonder - has the Aussie aura finally cracked?     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: Tony Johnson: Crusaders' Almighty Defence, Chiefs-Brumbies, & Mouth-Watering Final | Super Rugby 2025
summary: Tony Johnson is back on the DSPN, and he joins host Martin Devlin to review the Super Rugby Pacific semifinals. They dissect the Crusaders’ heroic defensive stand against the Blues - 203 tackles, 38 phases defended - and the red and blacks' legendary 31–0 home playoff record. Meanwhile, the Chiefs steamrolled the Brumbies, with Damian McKenzie starring and the bench making a huge impact. TJ and Marty discuss McKenzie's brilliance, and the Chiefs’ hunger to farewell coach Clayton McMillan in style. TJ highlights the crucial role of discipline, especially given the yellow card carnage from the 2023 final that hurt the Chiefs. They wrap by predicting a nail-biter final in Christchurch.     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

summary: Welcome to episode 97 of the DSPN - Devlin Sports Podcast Network...   Sky Sport rugby commentator Tony Johnson is back in the DSPN studio, reviewing the Super Rugby Pacific semifinals, that saw the Crusaders and Chiefs both win, and set-up a repeat of the 2023 decider. Marty and young producer Lachlan Waugh take on the Tight 5 once again, where they dissect and debate five separate sporting topics. They talk about the likes of Kiwi cricketer Finn Allen's monstrous knock, Shane van Gisbergen winning in NASCAR, and Auckland City's 10-0 loss to Bayern Munich in the FIFA Club World Cup. Tony is back to list the 10 most memorable Super Rugby finals alongside Marty. They talk about the 2007 all-South African nail-biter, the famous fog final, Craig Joubert's contentious call in 2014, and more.  All that and much more!     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: Jamie Wall: 1990s Blues, 2002 Crusaders, Late 2000s Bulls - Ranking Super Rugby's Greatest Ever Teams
summary: On the DSPN, Martin Devlin is joined by RNZ rugby reporter Jamie Wall, to dive headfirst into the ultimate bar-room rugby debate - the greatest Super Rugby teams of all time. Martin rattles off his top five, including the 1998–2000 Crusaders, the Bulls dynasty (2007–2010), and that ruthless 2012–13 Chiefs outfit. Jamie, while bravely setting aside his anti-Crusaders bias, throws in wildcards like the 2014 Waratahs and the near-miss Lions of 2016-2018. He passionately defends the early-2000s Hurricanes, citing a backline stacked with legends like Christian Cullen, Jonah Lomu, and Tana Umaga. They both agree on the greatness of the perfect 13-0 2002 Crusaders and the emotionally-charged 2011 side post-earthquake. The chat wraps with a preview of this weekend's semifinals between the Crusaders and Blues, and Chiefs and Brumbies.     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: Super Rugby's Greatest Teams, Jamie Wall, Mark Robinson's Resignation, Greg Martin | 12  June
summary: Welcome to episode 96 of the DSPN - Devlin Sports Podcast Network...   Martin Devlin kicks things off delivering a lengthy sermon about outgoing New Zealand Rugby CEO Mark Robinson. Marty describes Robinson as a lovely person who is likable and easy to get along with, but when it comes to his ability to lead NZ's biggest sporting body, he has been a failure. RNZ rugby reporter Jamie Wall previews the Super Rugby Pacific semifinals. More importantly though, he and Marty list their greatest EVER Super Rugby teams. Where does the 1996-97 Blues side rate? Can Jamie and Marty squeeze in a Hurricanes team? Greg Martin - former Wallabies fullback and currently a Triple M breakfast radio host in Brisbane - previews the Super Rugby weekend ahead, while touching on the NRL, swimming with crocodiles, and renovating his daughter's house.  All that and much more!     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

summary: In this heartfelt interview, DSPN host Martin Devlin is joined by All Blacks icon Murray Mexted to firstly reflect on the life and times of Stu Wilson, who earlier this week died aged 70. Mex, a long-time friend and teammate, recalls sharing a coffee and a laugh with Wilson just hours before his death, describing him as positive, and larger than life. Mex and Marty reflect on Wilson’s extraordinary rugby career, his elusive brilliance on the wing, uncanny anticipation, and game-changing presence. Then, the pair discuss the Super Rugby Pacific playoffs with a focus on the Blues and Crusaders' recent wins, and the semifinals coming up. Mex and Marty also touch on the All Blacks loose forwards and who they think should be starting at 6, 7 and 8 when the test season begins.     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: From Chaos to Contenders - Richie Barnett on NZ Warriors vs Sharks, & Club's Turnaround | 2025 NRL Season
summary: Richie Barnett - former Kiwis captain, and a man who once worse the Sydney Roosters and Cronulla Sharks strips - joins DSPN host Martin Devlin, to reflect on the Warriors’ stunning rise in 2025. Their NRL campaign started with a huge defeat to the Canberra Raiders, but since the Mount Smart club has garnered a 10-3 record and sits comfortably inside the NRL top four. Richie praises the squad’s identity, leadership, and consistency, highlighting halfback Luke Metcalf’s growth and the spine’s improvement. Richie also flags the importance of squad health heading into the finals in a few months' time, and with a six-point gap to fifth, the top four dream is looking very real. title: NRL Report Card: NZ Warriors & Raiders Flying, Roosters Lurking, & Can We Trust the Storm?
summary: On the DSPN, Zane The Warriorholic joins host Lachlan Waugh to break down the Warriors’ gritty rise in 2025. They have a 9-3 record on the NRL season, and travel across the ditch to face the Cronulla Sharks this weekend. Zane and Lachie talk about Mitch Barnett’s injury, the squad’s forward depth, and Jackson Ford’s transformation into a top-tier bench weapon. Zane reckons this year’s success is no fluke, with a rock-solid culture, high completion rates, and a “next man up” mentality that’s built to last. They also question whether there was an overreaction to the losses of Shaun Johnson and Tohu Harris - among others - ahead of the season.  Then, the boys hand out midseason report cards, starting with surprise packages like the Wahs and the Raiders (finally consistent!). The disappointments? Lachlan’s Broncos top the list, with Zane gleefully piling on. Melbourne also cops a serve despite their ladder spot, with both agreeing the Storm haven’t lived up to expectations. All of that and much more!     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

Kempy tips his hat to halfback Luke Metcalf's maturity in the seven jumper, though flags defensive work-ons with the likelihood of the Warriors playing finals footy growing. The pair chat about the Warriors’ top-four hopes, Penrith’s looming resurgence, and why the Broncos look completely lost. Kempy also vents about the dodgy world of player agents, calling for the NRL to introduce a bond system. title: 'Give the Highlanders a Chance!' - Super Rugby Pacific Preview, Warriors vs Rabbitohs, Broncos Bounce back? | What Has More Chance
summary: Buckle up, sports fan - DSPN host Martin Devlin and young producer Lachlan Waugh are back, laying a game off What Has More Chance Of Happening. Marty and Lachie debate which Super Rugby Pacific underdog might pull off a miracle (spoiler: probably not the Waratahs), they dish out NRL odds like seasoned bookies, and argue over who's got the better chance - Liam Lawson cracking the F1 top 10 or Max Verstappen doing Max Verstappen things. There's even some A-League chat and a love letter to greasy chips at Brookvale Oval. It’s full-throttle sport talk, served with a cheeky grin.     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: 'They've Earned it' - Clinton Toopi raves about Warriors' NRL Season, & Reviews Queensland's Clunky Performance in State of Origin Opener
summary: On the DSPN, Martin Devlin yarns with Clinton Toopi, the former Warriors and Kiwis centre, who dives into everything from State of Origin game one to Warriors' hopes for the 2025 NRL season. Toopi dishes on Queensland’s “clunky” misfires, NSW’s swagger, and why Origin ain't for the faint-hearted - or slow-reacting edge defenders. He also raves about Mitch Barnett’s madman ability to play back-to-back games like it’s a light jog. Off the field, Toopi’s been mentoring young rugby league guns on the Gold Coast, and promoting mental wellness - basically the NRL’s wise uncle. Finally, he tips his hat to the Warriors’ gritty rise, calling for consistency and dirty wins - because pretty footy doesn’t always get you the two points, but effort does!     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: Clinton Toopi, State of Origin, NZ Warriors, Greg Martin, Miles Davis | 29 May
summary: Welcome to episode 93 of the DSPN - Devlin Sports Podcast Network...   Former Warriors and Kiwis centre Clinton Toopi joins Marty on the podcast to review State of Origin game one in Brisbane, which New South Wales won comfortably 18-6. Clinton also casts his eye over the Warriors' 2025 NRL season, and talks about what he's up to now on the Gold Coast. Greg 'Marto' Martin from Triple M in Brisbane (also a former Wallabies fullback, by the way) provides a die-hard Queensland perspective on Origin. Marto also talks about a bunch of other sports coming up this weekend. Marty plays the Tight 5 with none other than Miles Davis! Mr Nonsense himself. The pair debate five different sporting topics that are doing the rounds in NZ and around the world at the moment, such as Run It Straight, the A-League grand final, and Super Rugby.  All that and much more!     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: 'They’re NOT Back Yet!' – Murray Mexted Questions Crusaders, Backs Hurricanes to Strike Big | Super Rugby 2025
summary: Former All Black Murray Mexted joins Martin Devlin on the DSPN, for a lively, no-holds-barred Super Rugby Pacific chat, mixing sharp analysis with classic Mexted humour. Murray's all in on the Chiefs, calling their unity under soon-to-depart coach Clayton McMillan “commendable,” and reckons the Hurricanes are peaking at just the right time with strike power all over the park. While he’s not convinced the Crusaders are fully “back", he gives them credit, and argues the Highlanders are better than their low ranking suggests - “a good team will always beat a team of good players.”  Mexted tips Fabian Holland as a future star, praises the leadership of Timoci Tavatavanawai and his rise from winger to midfield mainstay, and questions the inconsistency of the Blues, despite backing them to sneak into the playoffs. Through it all, he hammers one point home with classic Kiwi bluntness: it’s simple - win the ball, and you’ll win the game.     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: Tony Kemp: Warriors' 'Deserved the Win', Panthers & Bulldogs lose, State of Origin | 2025 NRL Season
summary: Tony Kemp - former Warriors coach, and ex Newcastle Knights and Kiwis player - is back on the DSPN alongside host Martin Devlin, to recap all the action from NRL round 12. It was a shortened round due to State of Origin, but featured a host of upsets. The Warriors fell at home to the Canberra Raiders 16-10 for their first defeat in over a month. First off, Marty sends some NRL quickfire questions Kempy's way, before the pair unpack the Warriors' loss, whether or not there was anything concerning about the performance, and how Canberra got the better of them. They move onto other NRL clubs such as the Canterbury Bulldogs and Penrith Panthers, and their current form in the midst of the Origin period. Finally, Kempy and Marty explore the upcoming challenges and fixtures for the Warriors, and give their thoughts on Queensland vs New South Wales.     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: Tony Kemp, NRL Glass Half Full, State of Origin, Joel Gould, Matt Gunn | 27 May
summary: Welcome to episode 92 of the DSPN - Devlin Sports Podcast Network...   Tony Kemp - former Warriors coach, and ex Newcastle Knights and Kiwis player - joins Martin Devlin as he does every week, to review the latest round in the NRL. Kempy and Marty spend much of their time talking about the Warriors' 16-10 loss to the Canberra Raiders, as well as previewing State of Origin. Marty and young producer Lachlan Waugh play a game of NRL Glass Half Full, where they offer their opinions on a number of clubs and fixtures at nearly the midway point of the 2025 season. Joel Gould from AAP in Brisbane previews Origin game one and all the key storylines attached to Wednesday's opener. Matt Gunn out of Twizel tries to be positive about his Bulldogs after their hammering at the hands of the Dolphins.  All that and much more!     Website: rova.nz/podcasts/dspn-devlin-sports-podcast-network  Instagram: @martindevlin_dspn  TikTok: @martindevlin_dspn  YouTube: @DevlinSportsPodcastNetwork

title: 'Dynamite!' - Tony Johnson on Chiefs' MASSIVE Win, Hurricanes & Crusaders' Form | Super Rugby 2025
summary: Tony Johnson - longtime Sky Sport rugby commentator - is back on the DSPN with host Martin Devlin, to review Super Rugby Pacific round 15 for 2025. What are the chances Moana Pasifika make the finals? Can they finish ahead of the Blues? They likely need to beat the in-form Hurricanes to do so.
